activities_projects.title: Identifying Types of Resources
steps: Teacher explains the concept of resources
Students list examples of natural, human-made, and human resources
Classify each example into the correct category
Discuss the importance of each type
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify different types of resources and their uses

activities_projects.title: Resource Sorting Activity
activities_projects.objective: Learn how resources are categorized based on origin
steps: Teacher prepares flash cards with names of resources
Students work in groups
Sort the resources into renewable and non-renewable categories
Paste them on chart paper and present to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the difference between renewable and non-renewable resources
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of sorting and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Map Activity: Natural Resources of India
activities_projects.objective: Locate major natural resources in India
steps: Provide a blank map of India
Mark regions rich in coal, iron, and petroleum
Label the locations clearly
Discuss the importance of these resources
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn the distribution of major natural resources in India

activities_projects.title: Poster on Resource Conservation
steps: Students design posters on saving natural resources
Include slogans about conservation
Add illustrations related to water, forests, and minerals
Display posters in the classroom
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop awareness about sustainable use of resources
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance of poster (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Group Discussion on Sustainable Development
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of sustainable development
steps: Divide the class into small groups
Discuss the meaning of sustainable development
List ways to conserve resources
Present ideas to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of using resources wisely
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Household Resource Survey
activities_projects.objective: Identify resources used in daily life
steps: Students list resources used at home
Classify them as natural or human-made
Identify which resources are renewable
Present findings in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect textbook concepts with real-life resource use

activities_projects.title: Resource Conservation Pledge
steps: Students write a pledge to conserve resources
Include actions like saving water and electricity
Share the pledge with classmates
Display pledges on the classroom board
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop responsibility toward protecting resources
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and commitment in pledge (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reading and Reflection on Resource Use
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the impact of resource overuse
steps: Read a section on resource depletion
Identify reasons for overuse of resources
Write a short reflection on conservation
Discuss answers in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the consequences of excessive resource use

activities_projects.title: Role Play on Resource Management
steps: Assign roles such as farmers, villagers, and government officials
Discuss problems caused by resource depletion
Suggest solutions for sustainable management
Act out the scenario
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn collaborative solutions for managing resources

activities_projects.title: Project: Resource Conservation at School
steps: Observe how water and electricity are used in school
Identify areas where resources are wasted
Suggest ways to reduce wastage
Prepare a report and present it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn practical ways to conserve resources
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of observation and suggestions (10 marks)
